commits.message:
mit-styling-aj1.7.8: the proof — a proofing blade pulls a draft into the specimen

Recast the wipe node from a generic pink/orange gradient with a glowing white
divider into THE PROOF: a constant-speed letterpress proofing blade that sweeps
a loose grey DRAFT of a word into the tightly-kerned, jade-inked finished
SPECIMEN on a baseline rule, with a jade wet-ink edge trailing the blade. A
linear travelling boundary between two full states — deliberately distinct from
mask's radial knockout.

- ONE scalar --s on the scene root drives BOTH CSS consumers (the specimen
  layer's clip AND the blade position) AND the readout [LAW:one-source-of-truth];
  the blade can never leave the reveal edge, the number can never disagree.
- bmf feedback honored ('before/after is boring — longer AND a twist'): longer
  via tempo 'movement' (the palette's longest breath); the twist is the
  print-proof reading — grey->jade ink, loose->tight tracking, baseline rule
  drawing in behind a wet-ink blade.
- Sweep is LINEAR in n.w with beat:'dwell' so the draft holds still through the
  read-beat, then a constant-speed blade (the honest press metaphor) stays
  aligned with the linear stepIdx captions (no easeOut front-load/desync).
- background:inherit makes the specimen layer opaque so the wipe occludes the
  wider loose-tracked draft instead of ghosting its outer letters through.
- Wears the shared specimen frame (8-strip crop-mark pseudo, positive inset),
  tracked-mono catalogue label, matte jade chrome, no bloom.
- Honest readout textContent-direct (no setText flicker); pure scroll-driven,
  no WAAPI -> REDUCE_MOTION via the global keyframe kill.

Verified in Chrome at 810px across the node's full active range: zero page
console errors (only the benign favicon 404), clip/blade/readout/captions all
agree, draft holds through the dwell, finished specimen stands clean.

mit-styling-aj1.7.7: the knockout — an aperture cut through the surface plate (#30)

Recast the mask node from a generic purple/jade radial reveal into THE
KNOCKOUT: a circular aperture cut through a matte SURFACE plate (graphite,
jade ink) to uncover the SUBSTRATE plate printed beneath it (jade, dark
negative ink) — the same word system in knockout. ONE growing hole IS the
reveal; the substrate was always printed there, the aperture only uncovers
it. Deliberately distinct from zoom's loupe (no magnification, no instrument
ring) and from parallax/snow depth (no planes, no particles).

One scalar drives the knockout radius, its jade cut-edge hairline, and the
honest APERTURE-% readout from one --r on the scene root [LAW:one-source-of-truth]
— the cut edge can never leave the hole, the number can never disagree with it.

bmf feedback honored (room to breathe): beat:'dwell' holds 'the surface'
fully legible through the read-beat, tempo bumped phrase->passage, and the
cut uses a quadratic ease-IN so the surface reads on past the dwell before the
knockout accelerates — aligning the three captions (whole -> cutting ->
beneath) to the picture instead of finishing the cut while still saying
'read it whole'.

Wears the gallery's print-specimen frame (8-strip crop-mark pseudo on .mk,
positive inset for the full-bleed scene; tracked mono catalogue label; flat
matte jade, no bloom). Pure scroll-driven, no WAAPI -> no extra REDUCE_MOTION
gating beyond the global keyframe kill.

Verified in Chrome across the node's full scroll range: zero console errors,
the surface holds at r=0 through the 26% dwell, then opens with caption and
readout tracking the radius at every sample.

ticket: mit-styling-aj1.7.7
